meta.title: Advanced Probability and Statistics Exam Prep

modules.title: Module 1: Probability
examFocus: Sample space, events, axioms of probability

topics.title: Sample Space, Events & Probability Axioms
topics.summary: The language of probability: experiments, outcomes, events, and the three Kolmogorov axioms.

topics.title: Probability Theorems & Counting
topics.summary: Computing probabilities using the addition rule, complement rule, and counting techniques.

topics.title: Conditional Probability & Independence
topics.summary: Updating probability with extra information; checking whether two events are independent.

topics.title: Bayes' Theorem
topics.summary: Reversing conditional probabilities; the formula and worked numerical problems.

modules.title: Module 2: Random Variables
examFocus: Probability Mass Function (PMF) and Probability Density Function (PDF)
Expectation, Variance, and Standard Deviation — calculations

topics.title: Discrete vs Continuous Random Variables
topics.summary: What a random variable is; the difference between discrete (PMF) and continuous (PDF) cases.

topics.title: PMF, PDF & CDF
topics.summary: The three core functions that describe a random variable and how to read each one.

topics.title: Expectation, Variance & Standard Deviation
topics.summary: Computing E(X), E(g(X)), Var(X) and SD for both discrete and continuous variables.

modules.title: Module 3: Statistical Distributions
examFocus: Choosing the right distribution for a problem

topics.title: Uniform, Bernoulli & Binomial
topics.summary: The first three distributions: when each applies, parameters, and the E(X)/Var(X) formulas.

topics.title: Geometric, Negative Binomial & Poisson
topics.summary: Distributions for counts of trials or rare events; PMFs, means and variances.

topics.title: Normal (Gaussian) Distribution
topics.summary: The Normal distribution: parameters, symmetry, empirical rule, and Z-score standardization.

modules.title: Module 4: Inferential Statistics I
examFocus: Hypothesis testing: H0/H1, Type I & II errors, 1- vs 2-tailed
Z-test for means: one-sample and two-sample
Z-test for proportions: one- and two-sample (two-sample proportion is IMP — 3 of 6 IA2 questions)

topics.title: Populations, Samples & Sampling
topics.summary: The inferential setup: from a population to a sample, and the role of parameters and statistics.

topics.title: Hypothesis Testing: H0, H1, Errors & P-value
topics.summary: Setting up H0 and H1, choosing alpha, computing the p-value, and making a decision.

topics.title: Z-Test: Means & Proportions
topics.summary: Large-sample Z-tests: one- and two-sample tests for means, plus one- and two-sample tests for proportions — the proportion tests are the most-asked type in IA.

modules.title: Module 5: Inferential Statistics II
examFocus: Effect size and reporting statistical findings

topics.title: T-Test: One-Sample, Two-Sample & Paired
topics.summary: When the population variance is unknown — T-test variants, formulas, and decision rules.

topics.title: F-Test for Equality of Variances
topics.summary: Comparing two variances using the F-distribution; when to use it and how to interpret the result.

topics.title: ANOVA: One-Way & Two-Factor
topics.summary: Comparing three or more means; F-statistic, p-value, and the meaning of a significant result.
